original: World's largest dinosaur footprint, measuring 1.7 meters, is found in Kimberley, Western Australia
Astrid Lindgrensprains ankle and begins writing "Pippi Longstocking"
Study of a 71 year-old British woman who has never felt pain, for the first time understood as due to a gene mutation, published in British Journal of Anaesthesia
"Greatest Show On Earth" formed byP. T. BarnumandJames Anthony Bailey
Michael Jordanscores 69 points, 4th time he scores 60 pts in a game
Robert Goddarduses gyroscopes to control a rocket
First seaplane takes off from water under its own power, piloted by Henri Fabre from the Étang de Berre lagoon in Martigues, France
English astronomer Fred Hoyle coins the term "Big Bang" in a BBC radio talk while actually arguing for the alternative "steady-state" theory
European parliament bans single-use plastics, including cutlery and straws by 2021
Madame Tussaud's Wax Museum in London unveils figures of The Beatles; the waxworks later appear on the Sgt. Pepper album cover
The 2005 Sumatran earthquake rocks Indonesia, and at magnitude 8.7 is the second strongest earthquake since 1960.
International Cometary Explorer measures solar wind ahead of Halley
Wilhelm Olbers discovers 2 Pallas, the second known asteroid
Marlin Perkins, American TV host (Mutual of Omaha's Wild Kingdom), born in Carthage, Missouri (d. 1986)
Galen Clark, Canadian-born American naturalist and conservationist (discovered and helped preserve Mariposa's Grove of giant Sequoia trees), born in Shipton, Canada East (d. 1910)[1]
US PresidentGeorge H. W. Bushposthumously awardsJesse Owensthe Congressional Gold Medal
The Louvre Pyramid designed byI. M. Peiis inaugurated by French President François Mitterrand in Paris
Nathaniel Briggs of New Hampshire patents a washing machine
First ambulance goes into service
Discovery of  Epstein-Barr virus, the first human tumor virus, identified by pathologists English Anthony Epstein and Yvonne Barr published in "Lancet"
Chris Sharmacompletes the first redpoint of Sleeping Lion in Siurana, Spain, a 9b+ (5.15c) route
Yevgeni Kulikov skates world record 500m (36.91 secs)
Sri Lankan cricket fast bowler Lasith Malinga produces unprecedented sequence of 4 wickets in 4 balls, as South Africa scrambles to a 1-wicket Super 8's ICC World Cup win in Guyana
Type II supernova detected in M81 (NGC 3031)
Michael W. Young, American geneticist noted for work studying circadian rhythms (Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, 2017), born in Miami, Florida
Wubbo Ockels, Dutch physicist and astronaut, born in Almelo Netherlands, (d. 2014)
Selma Rubin, American environmental activist (Earth Day co-founder), born in Toledo, Ohio (d. 2012)
Cyclone Debbie strikes north east Queensland coast as a category 4 storm
Britain introduces 1st new pound coin in 30 years with secret security feature inside to stop counterfeiting
Wilt Chamberlainplays his last pro basketball game
NHL Washington Capitals win 1st game on road after 37 straight road loses
NZ cricket all out for 26 v England at Eden Park
Nabisco Dinah Shore Women's Golf, Mission Hills CC: Helen Alfredsson of Sweden wins her only major title, 2 strokes ahead of 3 runners-up; first non-American to win the event as a major
Harvey Glance, American athlete (Olympic gold 4x100m relay 1976; World C'ship gold 4x100m relay 1987), born in Phenix City, Alabama (d. 2023)
Ronnie Ray Smith, American athlete, 4 X 100m relay runner (Olympic gold 1968), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2013)
Karen Kain, Canadian ballet dancer, 1973-97 (Moscow International Ballet Competition, 1973), and artistic director (National Ballet of Canada, 2005-21), born in Hamilton, Ontario
Chris Barrie [Brown], British impressionist, comic, and actor (Red Dwarf - "Arnold Rimmer"; The Brittas Empire), born in Hanover, West Germany
First true pirate radio station, England's Radio Caroline begins regular transmission at noon from the MV Caroline off the coast of Suffolk
Martina Navratilovabeats 16-year-old American tennis prodigyAndrea Jaeger6-3, 7-6 in the final of the WTA Tour Championships at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Louvre opens to the public (although officially opened since August)
Turkish cities Constantinople and Angora change their names to Istanbul and Ankara
Pope Francisbecomes the first Pope to wash the feet of women in the Maundy Thursday service
Elvis Presleyrecords his final Top Ten hit, a cover of "Burning Love", a song written by Dennis Linde, and first recorded by Arthur Alexander
First orchestral performance ofJohannes Brahms' Serenade No. 1

Jay Livingston, American Academy Award-winning composer and songwriter (chiefly with Ray Evans - "Que Será, Será", "Silver Bells"; "Mona Lisa"; "Tammy", theme to "Mr. Ed"), born in McDonald, Pennsylvania (d. 2001)[1]
Jimmie Dodd, American actor (The Mickey Mouse Club; Thundering Trails; Riders of the Rio Grande), and singer-songwriter, born in Cincinnati, Ohio (d. 1964)
Neville Bonner, the first Indigenous Australian to become a member of the Parliament of Australia, born in Ukerebagh Island, New South Wales (d. 1999)
Thad Jones, American jazz saxophonist, flugelhorn player, arranger, bandleader, and composer (Count Basie; Thad Jones/Mel Lewis Orchestra), born in Pontiac, Michigan (d. 1986)
Frederick "Freddie" Bartholomew, British child actor (Anna Karenina, David Copperfield, Captains Courageous), born in London, England (d. 1992)
Thomas Clarkson, English abolitionist (Negro Emancipation), born in Wisbech, Cambridgeshire, England (d. 1846)
Palm Sunday tornado outbreak of 1920 affects the Great Lakes region and Deep South states
27.9 cm precipitation at McMinnville, Tennessee (state record)
Stanley Cup Final, Mutual Street Arena, Toronto, ON: Toronto St Patricks (NHL) beat Vancouver Millionaires (PCHA), 5-1 for a 3-2 series win
AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament, Delta States beat Immaculata, 69-64 at Pennsylvania State University
Kraft Nabisco Championship Women's Golf, Mission Hills CC: Grace Park wins her only major title by 1 stroke, sinking a 6-foot birdie putt on the 72nd hole; fellow South Korean Aree Song is runner-up
Stacking of Discovery's SRBs gets underway

Majestic Theater opens at 245 W 44th St, NYC; designed with a modern Spanish exterior and French revival style interior by architect Herbert J. Krapp, it is largest of 3 theater and hotel development cuilt by Irving Chanin
Pope John appoints the first Japanese, African & Filipino cardinals
Juan Bautista de Anza finds the site for the Presidio of San Francisco
Foundation stone laid for the new city of La Valletta in Malta by Knights Hospitaller Grand Master Jean de Valette
